comment: Declarations that the unused-exports CI check should not flag. Each entry pairs a fully-qualified name with the reason it is exempt; `check_unused_exports` errors out if a listed name does not exist in the loaded environment, which keeps the file from going stale silently.

exemptions.name: Core.Dsl.IdentRefs.dslProofMarker
exemptions.reason: Identity-function placeholder emitted by the DSL into rendered Lean source. `graftDslProofMarkers` rewrites each `dslProofMarker (…)` placeholder back to the user's original `proof[…]` body before elaboration, so no Lean constant referrer is ever stored in the env; the function exists only because the placeholder must parse as a term.

exemptions.name: Core.Dsl.IdentRefs.resolveStructField
exemptions.reason: Struct-field resolver invoked from `run_cmd` smoke tests in `Tests/DslGotoDef.lean`. `run_cmd` blocks execute at elaboration time but do not produce stored Lean constants, so their references are invisible to `getUsedConstantsAsSet`. The production `↦` parser rule no longer calls this resolver (gotoDef goes through `recordStructFieldRef` + `graftLocalIdentsFromBuffers`), but the function still backs the regression tests that pin the registry's behaviour.

exemptions.name: DslExprF.embed
exemptions.reason: Auto-generated by the `derive_base_functor` macro as part of the standard recursion-scheme API (dual of `DslExpr.project`). The macro emits the full set of combinators for symmetry; this codebase consumes `cata` / `para` and their monadic variants, but `embed` is part of the published interface.

exemptions.name: registerPresentationDef
exemptions.reason: Public registration entry point for template `Presentation` values. Called from user-written templates via the `registerPresentation` syntax wrapper in `Core.Dsl.RegisterPresentation`, whose elab_rules emits a string-built `initialize registerPresentationDef …` at user call sites. The macro expansion is invisible to `getUsedConstantsAsSet` until a user defines a template, so the analyzer flags it as unused even when the wrapper is in active use.

exemptions.name: PresElement.defRef?
exemptions.reason: Public helper on the user-facing `PresElement` type used by template authors and by the template renderer (`Presentation.Template.buildTemplatePresentationLatex`). The renderer lives in the `Presentation` lib, which is not transitively imported by `Presentation.lean` itself (Template imports Presentation, not the other way around), so the check sees no Core-side referrer.

exemptions.name: PropertyDef.referencedNames
exemptions.reason: Per-kind dependency helper consumed by the template renderer's closure walker (`Registry.dependenciesOf` in `Presentation.Template`). Same story as `PresElement.defRef?`: the Template module isn't part of the Presentation root file's transitive import graph, so the analyzer can't see the call edge from `Core` to it. Mirrors the existing `*.referencedNames` API on FnDef / InductivePropertyDef.

exemptions.name: TheoremDef.referencedNames
exemptions.reason: Per-kind dependency helper consumed by the template renderer's closure walker (`Registry.dependenciesOf` in `Presentation.Template`). Same story as `PresElement.defRef?` / `PropertyDef.referencedNames`: the Template module isn't part of the Presentation root file's transitive import graph, so the analyzer can't see the call edge from `Core` to it.
